items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: IRGC reject Omani-IMO Hormuz transit framework
items.summary: Rupee set to rally at open as oil slides below pre-Iran war levels, bucking Asia weakness Australia jobs bounce masks part-time tilt and April revision as RBA August hold holds Bessent Greenspan "tap the brakes" remark read as green light for Warsh hike in September Australian May unemployment rate 4.4% (expected 4.4%, prior 4.5%) Iran's IRGC warns ships face action for using Hormuz routes not cleared with Tehran PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8209 (vs. estimate at 6.8048) BoJ Tamura says neutral rate around 2% and its important to get closer sooner JPMorgan says non-US stocks at 25% discount but cheap alone won't shift flows South Korea equities surging at the open, Kospi up more than 5% Magnitude 7.1 quake topples Caracas buildings as USGS warns of high Venezuela casualties UK car output snaps four-month slide in May but US surge masks deeper industry stress NY Fed's Marchioni calls new FOMC reserves language cleanup, not a Warsh policy shift MUFG says intervention threat slowing yen slide as BOJ hike fails to shift USD/JPY trend JP Morgan lifts S&P 500 target to 7,800 but warns of flash crash risk in crowded AI trades JP Morgan cuts Brent forecast to $78 year-end as demand miss and inventory lag bite investingLive Americas FX news wrap 24 Jun: Dollar rally extends as risk-off mood deepens Micron smashes estimates with $41.5B quarter, guides to $50B on AI memory surge Micron stock rips higher after reporting earnings Summary: IRGC Navy rejected the Omani-IMO Hormuz transit framework, calling the proposed route unacceptable and a serious safety risk, giving oil a brief pop though rising traffic through the strait remains the dominant price weight BOJ board member Tamura called for rate hikes every few months toward a 2% neutral rate, said underlying inflation has already reached 2%, and warned the BOJ should not hesitate to accelerate if upside price risks heighten Australia added 40,300 jobs in May and unemployment fell to 4.4%, but the April revision to minus 40,700, a part-time-heavy composition and a 1.1% fall in hours worked tempered the headline; household spending beat strongly at 1.3% against a 0.5% forecast with annual growth at 5.5% Japanese and South Korean equities climbed, led by tech after Micron's blowout earnings and guidance reignited AI trade confidence Successive earthquakes, a 7.1 followed by a 7.5 sixty seconds later, struck north-central Venezuela, causing severe damage across Caracas; a state of emergency has been declared and the main airport closed US Speaker Johnson meets President Trump at the White House at 2pm Eastern Thursday Iran's IRGC Navy injected fresh uncertainty into the Hormuz picture in the session, rejecting the transit framework proposed under Omani and International Maritime Organization auspices and declaring the route unacceptable and a serious safety risk. The language carries an implicit threat to resume pressure on commercial shipping or impede mine clearance, though the dominant oil market dynamic remains the steady increase in vessel traffic successfully transiting the strait, which kept the crude price reaction to a modest pop. BOJ board member Naoki Tamura delivered the session's most hawkish central bank signal, saying underlying inflation has already reached 2%, calling for rate hikes at intervals of a few months toward a neutral rate of around 2%, and warning the board should not hesitate to accelerate or move in larger increments if upside price risks intensify. The remarks reinforce the hawkish read from the June Summary of Opinions released Wednesday. Australia's May labour force data came in above the headline consensus at 40,300 jobs added and unemployment back at 4.4%, but the quality of the print was mixed: April was revised sharply to minus 40,700, most of May's gain was part-time, and hours worked fell 1.1%. Household spending provided the hawkish offset, beating expectations with a 1.3% monthly gain and 5.5% annual growth across all nine spending categories. AUD barely moved, and major FX was broadly subdued with the dollar edging only marginally lower. Japanese and South Korean equities climbed, led by technology names after Micron's record earnings and guidance reignited conviction in the AI trade and provided relief to a sector that had been watching for confirmation the demand cycle remains intact. Our thoughts are with everyone in Venezuela tonight. Successive earthquakes, a 7.1 followed by a 7.5 sixty seconds later, struck north-central Venezuela, causing devastating damage across Caracas. A state of emergency is in effect, the main airport has been closed after suffering severe damage, and metro and rail services have been suspended. The full, tragic, human toll is still emerging. Looking ahead, Speaker Johnson meets President Trump at the White House at 2pm Eastern Thursday. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 随着油价跌破伊朗战争前的水平，卢比开盘料将走强，逆亚洲市场疲软趋势而行；澳大利亚就业数据反弹掩盖了兼职比例上升及4月数据修正，澳大利亚储备银行8月维持利率不变；贝森特·格林斯潘“踩刹车”的言论被解读为沃什9月加息的绿灯；澳大利亚5月失业率为4.4%（预期4.4%，前值4.5%） 伊朗革命卫队警告，未经德黑兰批准使用霍尔木兹海峡航线的船只将面临行动 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8209 （市场预期为6.8048）日本央行田村表示中性利率约为2%，尽早向该水平靠拢至关重要；摩根大通称非美股市折价25%，但仅凭估值低廉不足以改变资金流向；韩国股市开盘大涨，KOSPI指数上涨逾5%；7.1级地震导致加拉加斯多栋建筑倒塌，美国地质调查局警告委内瑞拉伤亡人数可能很高；英国汽车产量终结了连续四 个月的下滑，但美国产量的激增掩盖了行业更深层次的压力 纽约联储的马尔基奥尼称，FOMC关于储备金的表述调整仅是措辞优化，并非沃什政策的转变 三菱日联金融集团表示，干预威胁正减缓日元贬值，因日本央行加息未能扭转美元兑日元走势 摩根大通将标普500指数目标上调至7,800点，但警告称拥挤的人工智能交易存在闪崩风险 摩根大通将布伦特原油年末预测下调至78美元 随着需求不及预期和库存滞后效应显现，investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述（6月24日）：避险情绪加深，美元涨势延续；美光科技季度营收达415亿美元，远超预期，受AI内存需求激增推动，预计全年营收将达500亿美元；美光科技公布财报后股价飙升。摘要： 伊朗革命卫队海军拒绝了阿曼与国际海事组织（IMO）达成的霍尔木兹海峡过境框架协议，称拟议航线不可接受且存在严重安全风险，此举虽使油价短暂上涨，但海峡过境流量的增加仍是影响油价的主要因素 日本央行理事田村呼吁每隔数月加息一次，以达到2%的中性利率，并表示核心通胀率已达到2%， 并警告称，若通胀上行风险加剧，日本央行应毫不犹豫地加快加息步伐。澳大利亚5月新增就业岗位40,300个，失业率降至4.4%，但4月数据修正为减少40,700个，加上就业结构以兼职为主以及工作时长下降1.1%，削弱了这一数据的积极影响； 家庭支出表现强劲，环比增长1.3%，远超0.5%的预期，同比增长5.5%。受美光科技（Micron）超预期财报及业绩指引提振，市场对人工智能板块的信心重燃，带动科技股领涨，日股和韩股随之走高。接连发生两次地震，先是7.1级，60秒后又发生7.5级， 飓风袭击了委内瑞拉中北部，给加拉加斯各地造成严重破坏；该国已宣布进入紧急状态，主要机场也已关闭。美国众议院议长约翰逊将于周四东部时间下午2点在白宫会见特朗普总统。伊朗革命卫队海军在此次会议上为霍尔木兹海峡局势增添了新的不确定性，拒绝了在阿曼和国际海事组织主持下提出的过境框架，并宣称该航线不可接受且存在严重安全风险。这一表态隐含着将重新对商业航运施压或阻碍扫雷工作的威胁，尽管石油市场的主导趋势仍是成功通过海峡的船舶交通量稳步增加，这使得原油价格的反应仅出现小幅上涨。日本央行理事田村直树在本次会议上发出了最鹰派的央行信号，他表示基本通胀率已达到2%，呼吁以数月为间隔加息，直至达到约2%的中性利率水平，并警告称，若通胀上行风险加剧，理事会不应犹豫，应加快加息步伐或加大加息幅度。这些言论强化了周三发布的6月《意见摘要》中所体现的鹰派基调。 澳大利亚5月劳动力市场数据表现优于市场普遍预期，新增就业岗位40,300个，失业率回落至4.4%，但数据质量喜忧参半：4月数据被大幅下修至减少40,700个岗位，5月新增就业岗位中大部分为兼职岗位，且工作时长下降了1.1%。家庭支出数据则提供了鹰派支撑，九大支出类别中月环比增长1.3%，同比增幅达5.5%，均超出预期。澳元汇率基本持平，主要货币普遍表现平淡，美元仅小幅走低。受美光科技创纪录的盈利和业绩指引提振，科技股领涨，重燃市场对人工智能板块的信心，并缓解了该行业此前对需求周期是否依然稳固的担忧，受此提振，日本和韩国股市均有所上涨。今晚，我们的心与委内瑞拉的每一位民众同在。委内瑞拉中北部接连发生两次地震，先是7.1级，60秒后又发生7.5级，给加拉加斯各地造成了毁灭性破坏。目前该国已进入紧急状态，主要机场因遭受严重破坏而关闭，地铁和铁路服务也已暂停。这场悲剧造成的全部人员伤亡情况仍在逐步浮出水面。 展望未来，众议院议长约翰逊将于周四东部时间下午2点在白宫会见特朗普总统。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹。

and a serious safety risk, giving oil a brief pop though rising traffic through the strait remains the dominant price weight BOJ board member Tamura called for rate hikes every few months toward a 2% neutral rate, said underlying inflation has already reached 2%, and warned the BOJ should not hesitate to accelerate if upside price risks heighten Australia added 40,300 jobs in May and unemployment fell to 4.4%, but the April revision to minus 40,700, a part-time-heavy composition and a 1.1% fall in hours worked tempered the headline; household spending beat strongly at 1.3% against a 0.5% forecast with annual growth at 5.5% Japanese and South Korean equities climbed, led by tech after Micron's blowout earnings and guidance reignited AI trade confidence Successive earthquakes, a 7.1 followed by a 7.5 sixty seconds later, struck north-central Venezuela, causing severe damage across Caracas; a state of emergency has been declared and the main airport closed US Speaker Johnson meets President Trump at the White House at 2pm Eastern Thursday Iran's IRGC Navy injected fresh uncertainty into the Hormuz picture in the session, rejecting the transit framework proposed under Omani and International Maritime Organization auspices and declaring the route unacceptable and a serious safety risk. The language carries an implicit threat to resume pressure on commercial shipping or impede mine clearance, though the dominant oil market dynamic remains the steady increase in vessel traffic successfully transiting the strait, which kept the crude price reaction to a modest pop. BOJ board member Naoki Tamura delivered the session's most hawkish central bank signal, saying underlying inflation has already reached 2%, calling for rate hikes at intervals of a few months toward a neutral rate of around 2%, and warning the board should not hesitate to accelerate or move in larger increments if upside price risks intensify. The remarks reinforce the hawkish read from the June Summary of Opinions released Wednesday. Australia's May labour force data came in above the headline consensus at 40,300 jobs added and unemployment back at 4.4%, but the quality of the print was mixed: April was revised sharply to minus 40,700, most of May's gain was part-time, and hours worked fell 1.1%. Household spending provided the hawkish offset, beating expectations with a 1.3% monthly gain and 5.5% annual growth across all nine spending categories. AUD barely moved, and major FX was broadly subdued with the dollar edging only marginally lower. Japanese and South Korean equities climbed, led by technology names after Micron's record earnings and guidance reignited conviction in the AI trade and provided relief to a sector that had been watching for confirmation the demand cycle remains intact. Our thoughts are with everyone in Venezuela tonight. Successive earthquakes, a 7.1 followed by a 7.5 sixty seconds later, struck north-central Venezuela, causing devastating damage across Caracas. A state of emergency is in effect, the main airport has been closed after suffering severe damage, and metro and rail services have been suspended. The full, tragic, human toll is still emerging. Looking ahead, Speaker Johnson meets President Trump at the White House at 2pm Eastern Thursday. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 24 Jun: Dollar rally extends as risk-off mood deepens
items.summary: Crude oil futures settle at $70.34. Stays below the 200 day MA. Hong Kong’s largest ETF is a leveraged bet on a single stock Bitcoin breaks $60,000 and falls to a 20-month low BOC minutes: Members agreed the economy was weak Micron earnings after the close. With price up 721% from a year ago, is it priced right? U.S. Treasury auctioned off 5 year note at a high yield of 4.20% Silver and Gold continue to cool off. Both trade at new 2026 lows Communication services soar: A glimpse into today's market dynamics US EIA weekly crude oil inventories -6088K vs -4461K expected US May new home sales 580K vs 639K expected Oil below $70 is one of the most-confounding market moves ever Life's Guarantees: Death, Taxes and the USDCAD moving higher Gold breaks $4000 for the first time since November US Q1 current account -$226.8 vs -$215 billion expected investingLive European markets wrap: Oil extends fall, US futures steady awaiting Micron The U.S. dollar continued its broad-based rally today as traders remained in a risk-off mood, favoring the greenback. Expectations for a Fed that may hike in 2026, along with equity markets which are vulnerable. The commodity and risk on currencies were the biggest losers ( AUD, NZD and CAD), while traditional safe havens like the Japanese yen held in. The USDJPY remains above the rising 100 hour moving average at 161.515, but below the 2024 high price at 161.95. Getting above that level would put the pair at the highest level going back to early 1987. NZDUSD: The New Zealand dollar was the weakest of the majors, falling 0.41% against the greenback, with the pair dropping to 0.5644 as traders continued to favor the dollar amid softer risk sentiment. AUDUSD: The Australian dollar declined 0.30% to 0.6893, extending its recent slide after mixed Australian inflation data and growing concerns over global growth and commodity demand. GBPUSD: Sterling slipped 0.30% to 1.3164, pressured by broad dollar strength and the market's preference for safer assets. Political uncertainty is a drag along with a stronger dollar. EURUSD: The euro fell 0.22% to 1.1356, despite a modest improvement in German business sentiment and continued hawkish comments from ECB officials. USDCAD: The Canadian dollar was one of the better performers, with the U.S. dollar rising only 0.18% to 1.4232. However, they price has been up for six consecutive days, and is up 660 pips over the last 38 trading days. USDJPY: The Japanese yen outperformed its G10 peers, with the dollar rising just 0.13% to 161.78. Safe-haven demand and lower U.S. yields helped cushion the yen despite the broader dollar rally. USDCHF: The Swiss franc was the weakest performer against the dollar, with USDCHF rising 0.32% to 0.8123 Overall, the dollar buying theme remained firmly intact, with the Dollar Index (DXY) holding near multi-year highs. Major US stock indices close mixed with a Dow industrial average higher while the S&P and the NASDAQ indices continued their weaker bias. Yesterday both of the broader indices fell below - and away from - their 100 and 200 hour moving averages. For the S&P, the 100 hour moving averages at 7444.93. The price is currently around 80 points away from that level as sellers take more control. For the NASDAQ index, its 100 hour moving averages at 26053. With the current price at 25476, there is 580 points between the moving average and the current price. A look at the closing level shows: Dow industrial average rose 184 points or 0.36% at 51856.18 S&P index fell -7.02 points or -0.10% at 7358.43 NASDAQ index -110.40 or -0.43% at 25476.64 The small-cap Russell 2000 eked out a small gain of 11.14 points or 0.37% to 2986.63. After the close, Micron beat on the top and bottom line with EPS coming in at $25.11 versus $20.78 expected. Revenues came in at $41.46 billion versus 35.85 million estimate. Shares are currently up 7.3% at $1124.50. That is still short of the all-time high price from Monday's trade at $1213.56 after declines over the last two trading days.. Gold, silver, crude oil, and Bitcoin are all under heavy pressure today as investors continue to unwind geopolitical and inflation hedges. Gold and silver have fallen to fresh 2026 lows as higher Treasury yields, a stronger U.S. dollar, and expectations for higher-for-longer Fed policy weigh on precious metals. Gold has broken below the key $4,000 level for the first time since November 2025, trading at $3,985.21, down $126 or 3.07%. Silver has been hit even harder, falling below $60 to $56.81, down $4.75 or 7.68%. Easing geopolitical tensions have reduced safe-haven demand, while concerns about slower global growth have added pressure to silver because of its industrial exposure. Technically, both metals remain below key moving averages, keeping the bias tilted to the downside. Crude oil has also seen a dramatic reversal, briefly falling below $70 per barrel for the first time since the start of the Iran war before rebounding modestly. WTI is currently trading near $70, down $3.20 or 4.40%, as traders strip out the geopolitical risk premium that had been built into prices. Improving tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, optimism over a U.S.-Iran peace framework, and expectations for additional Iranian supply have shifted the focus away from supply disruptions and toward softer demand and the potential for an oversupplied market later this year. Bitcoin remains under intense pressure after breaking below the key $60,000 level and falling to a roughly 20-month low of $59,018 before recovering slightly to $59,706. The selloff has been fueled by weaker institutional demand, persistent ETF outflows, and a move away from speculative assets as investors rotate toward equities and AI-related opportunities. Higher yields have also reduced the appeal of non-yielding assets like cryptocurrencies. Technically, the break below $60,000 is a significant blow for the bulls, with traders now eyeing downside targets near $52,500, $49,600, and $39,500. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 原油期货收于70.34美元，仍低于200日均线。香港最大ETF是针对单一股票的杠杆投资 比特币突破60,000美元后跌至20个月低点 加拿大央行会议纪要：委员们一致认为经济疲软 美光科技盘后公布财报。股价较一年前上涨721%，估值是否合理？ 美国财政部拍卖5年期国债，收益率高达4.20% 白银和黄金持续回调。两者均创下2026年新低 通信服务板块飙升：一窥今日市场动态 美国能源信息署（EIA）每周原油库存 -6088K，预期为 -4461K 美国5月新屋销售 580K，预期为 639K 油价跌破70美元是史上最令人费解的市场走势之一 生活的保障： 死亡、税收以及美元兑加元走高 黄金自11月以来首次突破4000美元 美国第一季度经常账户赤字为226.8亿美元，预期为2150亿美元 investingLive欧洲市场综述：油价延续跌势，美股期货持稳等待美光科技财报 由于交易员仍处于规避风险的情绪中，青睐美元，美元今日延续了全面反弹的势头。市场预期美联储可能在2026年加息，加之股市走势脆弱。 大宗商品货币和风险偏好型货币（澳元、新西兰元和加元）跌幅最大，而日元等传统避险货币则表现坚挺。美元兑日元仍维持在161.515的上升中100小时移动均线上方，但低于2024年高点161.95。若能突破该水平，该货币对将升至1987年初以来的最高点。NZDUSD：新西兰元在主要货币中表现最弱，兑美元下跌0.41%，汇价跌至0.5644，因风险偏好减弱，交易员继续青睐美元。澳元兑美元（AUDUSD）：受澳大利亚通胀数据喜忧参半以及市场对全球经济增长和大宗商品需求担忧加剧的影响，澳元下跌0.30%至0.6893，延续了近期跌势。英镑兑美元（GBPUSD）：受美元全面走强及市场偏好避险资产的压力，英镑下跌0.30%至1.3164。政治不确定性与美元走强共同拖累了汇价。欧元兑美元（EURUSD）：尽管德国商业信心略有改善，且欧洲央行官员继续发表鹰派言论，但欧元仍下跌0.22%，报1.1356。美元兑加元（USDCAD）：加元表现相对较佳，美元仅上涨0.18%，报1.4232。 然而，该汇率已连续六日上涨，在过去38个交易日中累计上涨660点。美元兑日元（USDJPY）：日元表现优于其他G10货币，美元仅上涨0.13%，报161.78。尽管美元整体走强，但避险需求和美国国债收益率走低帮助日元抵消了部分压力。美元/瑞郎：瑞士法郎对美元表现最弱，美元/瑞郎上涨0.32%至0.8123。总体而言，美元买盘趋势依然稳固，美元指数（DXY）维持在多年高位附近。美国主要股指收盘涨跌不一，道琼斯工业平均指数走高，而标普500指数和纳斯达克指数则延续了疲软态势。昨日，这两大指数均跌破并远离其100小时和200小时移动平均线。对于标普500指数而言，100小时移动平均线位于7444.93点。随着卖方占据上风，当前价格距该水平约有80点之差。纳斯达克指数的100小时移动平均线位于26053点。当前价格为25476点，移动平均线与当前价格之间相差580点。 从收盘水平来看：道琼斯工业平均指数上涨184点，涨幅0.36%，收于51856.18点；标普500指数下跌7.02点，跌幅0.10%，收于7358.43点；纳斯达克指数下跌110.40点，跌幅0.43%，收于25476. 64 罗素2000小盘股指数微涨11.14点，涨幅0.37%，收于2986.63点。收盘后，美光科技（Micron）营收和净利润均超出预期，每股收益（EPS）达25.11美元，高于预期的20.78美元。营收为414.6亿美元，高于3585万美元的预期。该股目前上涨7.3%，报1124.50美元。尽管过去两个交易日出现下跌，但该股价仍低于周一创下的1213.56美元历史高点。今日，随着投资者持续减持地缘政治和通胀对冲头寸，黄金、白银、原油和比特币均面临巨大压力。受美国国债收益率上升、美元走强以及市场预期美联储将长期维持高利率政策等因素影响，黄金和白银价格已跌至2026年以来的新低。黄金自2025年11月以来首次跌破关键的4,000美元关口，报3,985.21美元，下跌126美元，跌幅3.07%。白银跌幅更为剧烈，跌破60美元至56.81美元，下跌4.75美元，跌幅7.68%。 地缘政治紧张局势的缓和削弱了避险需求，而对全球经济增长放缓的担忧则因其工业用途，给白银价格带来了额外压力。从技术面看，这两种金属的价格仍低于关键移动平均线，因此走势仍偏向下行。原油价格也出现了剧烈反转，自伊朗战争爆发以来首次短暂跌破每桶70美元，随后小幅反弹。目前，WTI原油价格徘徊在70美元附近，下跌3.20美元，跌幅4.40%，因交易员正从价格中剔除此前已计入的地缘政治风险溢价。霍尔木兹海峡油轮航运状况好转、市场对美伊和平框架持乐观态度，以及对伊朗增产的预期，使市场关注点从供应中断转向需求疲软及今年晚些时候市场可能出现的供过于求局面。比特币在跌破关键的60,000美元关口后仍面临巨大压力，一度跌至约20个月低点59,018美元，随后小幅回升至59,706美元。 此轮抛售主要源于机构需求疲软、ETF资金持续流出，以及投资者转向股票和人工智能相关投资机会，从而撤离投机性资产。收益率的上升也降低了加密货币等无收益资产的吸引力。从技术面来看，跌破60,000美元对多头而言是一次重大打击，交易员目前正关注52,500美元、49,600美元和39,500美元附近的下行目标位。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基（Greg Michalowski）。
items.summaryEn: Crude oil futures settle at $70.34. Stays below the 200 day MA. Hong Kong’s largest ETF is a leveraged bet on a single stock Bitcoin breaks $60,000 and falls to a 20-month low BOC minutes: Members agreed the economy was weak Micron earnings after the close. With price up 721% from a year ago, is it priced right? U.S. Treasury auctioned off 5 year note at a high yield of 4.20% Silver and Gold continue to cool off. Both trade at new 2026 lows Communication services soar: A glimpse into today's market dynamics US EIA weekly crude oil inventories -6088K vs -4461K expected US May new home sales 580K vs 639K expected Oil below $70 is one of the most-confounding market moves ever Life's Guarantees: Death, Taxes and the USDCAD moving higher Gold breaks $4000 for the first time since November US Q1 current account -$226.8 vs -$215 billion expected investingLive European markets wrap: Oil extends fall, US futures steady awaiting Micron The U.S. dollar continued its broad-based rally today as traders remained in a risk-off mood, favoring the greenback. Expectations for a Fed that may hike in 2026, along with equity markets which are vulnerable. The commodity and risk on currencies were the biggest losers ( AUD, NZD and CAD), while traditional safe havens like the Japanese yen held in. The USDJPY remains above the rising 100 hour moving average at 161.515, but below the 2024 high price at 161.95. Getting above that level would put the pair at the highest level going back to early 1987. NZDUSD: The New Zealand dollar was the weakest of the majors, falling 0.41% against the greenback, with the pair dropping to 0.5644 as traders continued to favor the dollar amid softer risk sentiment. AUDUSD: The Australian dollar declined 0.30% to 0.6893, extending its recent slide after mixed Australian inflation data and growing concerns over global growth and commodity demand. GBPUSD: Sterling slipped 0.30% to 1.3164, pressured by broad dollar strength and the market's preference for safer assets. Political uncertainty is a drag along with a stronger dollar. EURUSD: The euro fell 0.22% to 1.1356, despite a modest improvement in German business sentiment and continued hawkish comments from ECB officials. USDCAD: The Canadian dollar was one of the better performers, with the U.S. dollar rising only 0.18% to 1.4232. However, they price has been up for six consecutive days, and is up 660 pips over the last 38 trading days. USDJPY: The Japanese yen outperformed its G10 peers, with the dollar rising just 0.13% to 161.78. Safe-haven demand and lower U.S. yields helped cushion the yen despite the broader dollar rally. USDCHF: The Swiss franc was the weakest performer against the dollar, with USDCHF rising 0.32% to 0.8123 Overall, the dollar buying theme remained firmly intact, with the Dollar Index (DXY) holding near multi-year highs. Major US stock indices close mixed with a Dow industrial average higher while the S&P and the NASDAQ indices continued their weaker bias. Yesterday both of the broader indices fell below - and away from - their 100 and 200 hour moving averages. For the S&P, the 100 hour moving averages at 7444.93. The price is currently around 80 points away from that level as sellers take more control. For the NASDAQ index, its 100 hour moving averages at 26053. With the current price at 25476, there is 580 points between the moving average and the current price. A look at the closing level shows: Dow industrial average rose 184 points or 0.36% at 51856.18 S&P index fell -7.02 points or -0.10% at 7358.43 NASDAQ index -110.40 or -0.43% at 25476.64 The small-cap Russell 2000 eked out a small gain of 11.14 points or 0.37% to 2986.63. After the close, Micron beat on the top and bottom line with EPS coming in at $25.11 versus $20.78 expected. Revenues came in at $41.46 billion versus 35.85 million estimate. Shares are currently up 7.3% at $1124.50. That is still short of the all-time high price from Monday's trade at $1213.56 after declines over the last two trading days.. Gold, silver, crude oil, and Bitcoin are all under heavy pressure today as investors continue to unwind geopolitical and inflation hedges. Gold and silver have fallen to fresh 2026 lows as higher Treasury yields, a stronger U.S. dollar, and expectations for higher-for-longer Fed policy weigh on precious metals. Gold has broken below the key $4,000 level for the first time since November 2025, trading at $3,985.21, down $126 or 3.07%. Silver has been hit even harder, falling below $60 to $56.81, down $4.75 or 7.68%. Easing geopolitical tensions have reduced safe-haven demand, while concerns about slower global growth have added pressure to silver because of its industrial exposure. Technically, both metals remain below key moving averages, keeping the bias tilted to the downside. Crude oil has also seen a dramatic reversal, briefly falling below $70 per barrel for the first time since the start of the Iran war before rebounding modestly. WTI is currently trading near $70, down $3.20 or 4.40%, as traders strip out the geopolitical risk premium that had been built into prices. Improving tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, optimism over a U.S.-Iran peace framework, and expectations for additional Iranian supply have shifted the focus away from supply disruptions and toward softer demand and the potential for an oversupplied market later this year. Bitcoin remains under intense pressure after breaking below the key $60,000 level and falling to a roughly 20-month low of $59,018 before recovering slightly to $59,706. The selloff has been fueled by weaker institutional demand, persistent ETF outflows, and a move away from speculative assets as investors rotate toward equities and AI-related opportunities. Higher yields have also reduced the appeal of non-yielding assets like cryptocurrencies. Technically, the break below $60,000 is a significant blow for the bulls, with traders now eyeing downside targets near $52,500, $49,600, and $39,500. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive European markets wrap: Oil extends fall, US futures steady awaiting Micron
items.summary: Headlines: Trump reaffirms that no money has been given to Iran yet and Hormuz will be toll-free Pakistan says US-Iran technical talks to only resume next week Qatar says LNG production to resume within a few weeks, eyes Strait of Hormuz reopening Oil prices are almost back to pre-war levels amid Hormuz normalisation, Fed hike risks US futures keep steadier so far today but the real test will come after the closing bell BOJ governor Ueda expects further interest rate hikes as underlying inflation picks up SNB policymaker Tschudin says medium-term inflation pressures are unchanged RBA's Hauser: We still have work to do to reduce inflation which remains far too high Germany Ifo business sentiment rebounds further in June Markets: WTI crude down 2.8% to $71.11 European equities mixed S&P 500 futures up 0.3%, Nasdaq futures up 0.6% USD leads, NZD lags on the day US 10-year yields down 3.5 bps to 4.46% Gold down 1.6% to $4,040 Bitcoin up 0.6% to $62,788 It was a steady session for the most part, with little notable headlines to really shake things up. The US and Iran are keeping the status quo, pending further technical talks next week. In the meantime, eyes will be on the Strait of Hormuz and how vessel traffic will pick up in the days ahead. The early indications are encouraging but is still very much limited for now. That is keeping oil prices subdued though, with WTI crude down nearly 3% to $71.11 as it moves closer to making the round trip back down. Besides that, European stocks are more mixed today while US futures are steadier with investors eyeing Micron earnings after the close. That will be the key signal for equities and tech shares especially as we look to the second half of the week. In other markets, the dollar continues to move from strength to strength as it pushes another advance today. EUR/USD is down 0.4% to 1.1340 and USD/JPY continues to flirt with the 2024 highs at 161.70 on the day. Meanwhile, AUD/USD is down 0.2% to 0.6898 after the sharp break below 0.7000 yesterday. As for precious metals, gold and silver are not finding much comfort from the risk selloff this week. The former is down 1.6% to $4,040 with the latter down over 4% to $59.30 on the day. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条新闻：特朗普重申尚未向伊朗提供任何资金，霍尔木兹海峡将保持畅通；巴基斯坦表示美伊技术磋商将于下周恢复；卡塔尔称液化天然气生产将在数周内恢复，并期待霍尔木兹海峡重新开放；随着霍尔木兹海峡局势趋于正常，油价已几乎回升至战前水平， 美联储加息风险 今日美股期货迄今走势平稳，但真正的考验将在收盘后到来 日本央行行长上田预计，随着核心通胀回升，将进一步加息 瑞士央行决策者楚丁表示，中期通胀压力保持不变 澳大利亚央行行长豪泽： “通胀水平依然过高，我们仍有工作要做以抑制通胀” 德国6月Ifo商业景气指数进一步回升 市场：WTI原油下跌2.8%至71.11美元 欧洲股市涨跌不一 标普500指数期货上涨0.3%， 纳斯达克期货上涨0.6%。当日美元领涨，新西兰元表现落后。美国10年期国债收益率下跌3.5个基点至4.46%。黄金下跌1.6%至4,040美元。比特币上涨0.6%至62,788美元。整体而言，今日行情平稳，鲜有足以引发市场剧烈波动的重大消息。美国与伊朗维持现状，等待下周进一步的技术性谈判。 与此同时，市场将密切关注霍尔木兹海峡的动向，以及未来几天船舶交通量将如何回升。初步迹象令人鼓舞，但目前规模仍非常有限。不过，这使得油价保持低迷，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）下跌近3%，至71.11美元，正逐渐接近完成一轮下跌后的反弹。此外，今日欧洲股市涨跌不一，而美国股指期货走势较为平稳，投资者正密切关注美光科技收盘后的财报。这将成为股市，尤其是科技股的关键信号，特别是展望本周后半段时。在其他市场方面，美元持续走强，今日再度上涨。欧元兑美元下跌0.4%至1.1340，美元兑日元则继续在当日触及161.70的2024年高点附近徘徊。与此同时，澳元兑美元在昨日急剧跌破0.7000关口后，今日下跌0.2%至0.6898。至于贵金属，黄金和白银本周并未从风险资产抛售潮中获得太多支撑。黄金当日下跌1.6%至4,040美元，白银则下跌逾4%至59.30美元。本文作者为investinglive.com的Justin Low。

The early indications are encouraging but is still very much limited for now.
